title: /Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/.claude 内容を表示せよ
summary_text: - `gh: Not Found (HTTP 404)` on the first reconcile check must be treated as **transient/eventual-consistency**, not failure. - Retry policy: on 404 or missing file, do **4 bounded retries** with backoff: `10s -> 30s -> 120s -> 600s`, then stop retries and mark as “pending_reconcile” for operator follow-up. - Recheck order each retry: 1) verify `gha_mirror_path` is still exactly the same canonical path from payload, 2) fetch mirror JSON, validate `record_id`, `status`, `run_id`, `run_attempt` shape, 3) verify `gha_receipt_path` exists and parse receipt metadata, 4) compare `record_id` + `run_id` + `run_attempt` consistency across mirror/receipt. - If mismatch vs exact path mismatch occurs, escalate immediately (not retry indefinitely): set hard-stop reason `reconcile_mismatch`. - Exit semantics: - `0`: reconciliation complete (both files present and validated). - `1`: retryable miss (`404` / not propagated yet); keep state `pending_reconcile` and reschedule. - `2`: hard failure (schema/path/token mismatch, parse failure, invalid provenance). - `3`: retries exhausted after bounded attempts; keep pending with “awaited propagation beyond threshold” reason for manual/cron recovery. - Journal/evidence should log: - attempt count, last 404 timestamp, chosen backoff, and exact reason string (`mirror_not_found`, `receipt_not_found`, `content_mismatch`), so operators can distinguish infra lag vs data corruption.
